items.title: Men tear gassed in Darwin's youth prison have payout slashed by High Court

children.content: The High Court slashes an award for exemplary damages for four former inmates from Darwin's notorious Don Dale Youth Detention Centre who were affected by a tear gas incident in 2014.

gallery.altText: The exterior of the Don Dale Youth Detention Centre, with barbed wire in the foreground.

thumbnail.altText: The exterior of the Don Dale Youth Detention Centre, with barbed wire in the foreground.

items.title: Unions and Inpex reach deal after weeks of strikes and negotiation

children.content: Workers at the Northern Territory's Inpex Ichthys facility have restarted regular duties after unions and the Japanese-owned company struck a deal to end a dispute that threatened to disrupt LNG exports.

image.altText: Inpex's Ichthys LNG Plant is on Darwin Harbour.

items.title: Women forced to labour in tearoom, run-down wards at NT's busiest hospital

children.content: From a mother forced to labour in a hospital's tearoom, to a baby sent home with jaundice on a busy night, doctors warn they are no longer able to provide NT patients with the standard of care they deserve.

image.altText: A woman with dark hair, holding a baby who is facing away while she looks right at the camera.

items.title: Alice Springs Victims of Crimes branch to close amid funding insecurity

children.content: The Alice Springs branch of Victims of Crime NT will be closed by the end of the week, with the organisation's chief executive citing funding insecurity. The closure will mean victims in the region will only be able to access services remotely.

gallery.altText: Man with gray moustache / beard, serious expression, sitting in an office, wearing a navy blue polo shirt.

thumbnail.altText: Man with gray moustache / beard, serious expression, sitting in an office, wearing a navy blue polo shirt.

items.title: Bluey speaks Indigenous language in time for NAIDOC week

children.content: The animated award-winning cartoon series Bluey has been translated into Yolŋu Matha, from north-east Arnhem Land, for NAIDOC Week.

image.altText: Bluey is sitting at the beach playing with a shell.

items.title: Roadside reptile mystery continues as remains confirmed as saltwater croc

children.content: Reptile remains found dumped on the side of an Adelaide roadside have been confirmed as belonging to a saltwater crocodile, but there is still no explanation for how they came to be there.

image.altText: crocodile with its mouth open on a muddy river bank.

items.title: 38yo man jailed for life over 'highly brutal murder' of 22yo wife in NT

children.content: Braden Jentian, 38, has been sentenced to life behind bars for the "protracted and highly brutal murder" of his 22-year-old female partner, whose body was found in a vacant block in Katherine in 2024.

image.altText: A white, modernist, flat-fronted building with "supreme court" in capital letters above a wide entrance.

items.title: Angus Taylor visits Alice Springs, renews call for Indigenous funding audit

children.content: Federal Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has visited Alice Springs, where he has met family of Kumanjayi Little Baby and renewed the Coalition's calls for deeper scrutiny of federal funding directed to the region.

image.altText: Taylor and Price memorial

images.altText: Lionel Messi holds his hands out wide

articles.title: 'We don't take s***': American star's ominous warning to Socceroos

children.content: American midfielder Sebastian Berhalter sends a tough warning to the Socceroos after a physical encounter between the US and Australia in a friendly last year. The two teams meet again on Saturday morning.

image.altText: A man in an American soccer jersey claps at the crowd.

articles.title: A-League referee's 'nonsense' decision denies Mbappe penalty

children.content: Kylian Mbappé scores two more World Cup goals to bring his overall tally to 14, just two shy of the all time record. But the biggest talking point comes when A-League referee Alireza Faghani denies the French superstar a penalty, with the call slammed by experts.

image.altText: Mbappé talks to Alireza Faghani

children.content: Lionel Messi scores the opener as Argentina takes on Algeria, after Erling Haaland scored twice as Norway beat Iraq 4-1 in Boston and Kylian Mbappé's super strike sunk Senegal. Follow live.

items.title: Messi adds another magic moment as Argentina leads Algeria

updates.title: Kick off: Argentina vs Algeria underway
Pulisic 'day to day' ahead of crucial Socceroos-USA clash

items.title: ASIC launches Zone RV criminal investigation over caravan company's collapse

children.content: The former director of Zone RV faces a criminal probe into allegations he acted recklessly or dishonestly before the manufacturer collapsed with debts of $42 million.

image.altText: caravans in a row

items.title: ASX slips, oil plunges to three-month low on reports US to scale back Iran sanctions

children.content: Oil prices sink to a three-month low on reports the US will allow Iran to start selling oil again, while the Australian share market falls after a sell-off across Wall Street's tech and AI stocks. Follow live.

image.altText: The ASX building exterior.

items.title: 'I didn't have a life': Pensioner moves to Thailand for cheaper living

children.content: Peter Stevenson says moving to Thailand has allowed him to live more comfortably on his Australian Age Pension after struggling to cover rent and essentials in regional Victoria.

gallery.altText: A selfie of a smiling man in silver-rimmed glasses and a grey tee taken from inside a restauraunt with red lanterns.

thumbnail.altText: A selfie of a smiling man in silver-rimmed glasses and a grey tee taken from inside a restauraunt with red lanterns.

items.title: Government to extend pay subsidy to avoid childcare worker walk-off

children.content: Early educators had threatened to walk off the job to keep a federally funded 15 per cent pay subsidy due to end in November.

image.altText: Unidentified child's hands playing with building bricks.

items.title: Pauline Hanson to address National Press Club for first time

children.content: Pauline Hanson will speak at the National Press Club for the first time since her entrance to politics in 1996. Follow live.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson and Cory Bernardi 2026-05-05 09:05:00

items.title: Marauding cockatoos thwarted by bin lid invented by men's shed

children.content: Residents of the scenic Great Ocean Road are at their wits' end after the increasingly intelligent birds learnt how to lift the lids of wheelie bins, spreading rubbish through the town of Lorne.

gallery.altText: A white cockatoo sits on top of a wooden table beside cutlery, sauce and a sign that says "cocktails".
Rubbish is scattered around four open blue and red wheelie bins where cockatoos sit in a car park on a cloudy day.
About 10 white cockatoos sit on wooden table tops and seats outside a cafe.

thumbnail.altText: A white cockatoo sits on top of a wooden table beside cutlery, sauce and a sign that says "cocktails".
Rubbish is scattered around four open blue and red wheelie bins where cockatoos sit in a car park on a cloudy day.
About 10 white cockatoos sit on wooden table tops and seats outside a cafe.

items.title: Why the market thinks Michele Bullock is bluffing on rate hikes

children.content: Michele Bullock made it abundantly clear that the RBA was not ruling out further interest rate rises, but the market appears to be unswayed by the tough talk.

image.altText: A dark-haired woman wearing glasses and a blazer leans on a lectern in front of an RBA-branded backdrop.

items.title: Gout runs a stunning third as Lyles breaks world record

children.content: Rising Australian superstar Gout Gout pushes American champion Noah Lyles all the way in their first race against each other.

image.altText: Two men hug after a running race.

items.title: What happened when Ann Patchett quit swearing for good

children.content: The bestselling author of books including Bel Canto, Tom Lake and her latest, Whistler, is determined to see the good in everyone.

gallery.altText: Ann Patchett, a woman with short greying hair, wearing a floral dress, sitting in front a series of bookshelves.

thumbnail.altText: Ann Patchett, a woman with short greying hair, wearing a floral dress, sitting in front a series of bookshelves.

items.title: Six gaming experts share their favourite video games of the year so far

children.content: From cosy builders to hectic shooters and all the way through to nostalgic adventures, there are lots of gems to enjoy on your favourite game console so far this year.

image.altText: Three people shaped characters stand with a pokemon trubbish

items.title: Bluey speaks Indigenous language in time for NAIDOC week

children.content: The animated award-winning cartoon series Bluey has been translated into Yolŋu Matha, from north-east Arnhem Land, for NAIDOC Week.

image.altText: Bluey is sitting at the beach playing with a shell.

items.title: Tim Wilson holds undisclosed shares in climate advisory firm through family trust

children.content: The shadow treasurer's investment trust holds 15,000 shares in a small company called Gondwana Carbon which helps developers to acquire land, develop carbon abatement projects, earn carbon credits and then trade them.

image.altText: Tim Wilson at National Press Club

items.title: Prince George heading to Eton College to continue education

children.content: Prince George, who turns 13 in July, will attend Eton College this September, following in the footsteps of his father, Prince William.

image.altText: Prince George

items.title: After the first attempt failed, outback town gives renewables another go

children.content: This outback town is giving renewable energy another shot as a new solar farm and battery system hooks up to the grid, two decades after a solar array project failed to live up to the hype.

gallery.altText: Hundreds of solar panels in a field.

thumbnail.altText: Hundreds of solar panels in a field.

items.title: Advocates call for a rethink on the factors driving male suicide

children.content: There are urgent calls for more customised support services for men and boys experiencing situational distress.

image.altText: close uup of Paul Withall

items.title: Police say man thankful after drug arrest halts lethal mushroom mix-up

children.content: A man dodges potentially fatal poisoning after allegedly mistaking lethal death cap mushrooms for their hallucinogenic counterparts while illegally foraging in southern Western Australia.

image.altText: Supplied photo of a poisonous mushroom seized by WA Police.

items.title: 'Unfair on him': Origin coaches back ref after gambling history revealed

children.content: Billy Slater and Laurie Daley come to the defence of referee Ashley Klein after his gambling debt history was revealed earlier in the week.

articles.title: State of Origin quiz: Tackle our More or Less challenge ahead of Game II

children.content: Today's ABC More or Less quiz challenges you to go deep into AFL territory and come away with the win. Mouthguards in, let's go.

image.altText: New South Wales and Queensland players fight

articles.title: What time is State of Origin Game II tonight?

children.content: NSW and Queensland meet at the famous MCG in State of Origin Game II. Here is what you need to know ahead of tonight's match.

image.altText: Reuben Cotter of the Maroons is tackled by Isaah Yeo and Hudson Young of the Blues

gallery.altText: An referee points at a player to leave the field.

thumbnail.altText: An referee points at a player to leave the field.

items.title: Dealing with NDIS leading to anxiety and suicidal ideation among participants

children.content: Participants say they are "exhausted" and "overwhelmed" by trying to navigate the complex and ever-changing NDIS system.

image.altText: Andrew Bretherton dressed in pants and dark jumper and jacket sits in a wheelchair.

items.title: Qld government accused of campaign to remove Aboriginal people from health roles

children.content: The Queensland government will not deny the existence of "Project Invisibility", a name a barrister says is used internally to refer to the deliberate removal of Aboriginal people from leadership roles.

image.altText: An Indigenous man speaking while standing in front of Aboriginal and Australian flags.

items.title: The councillor, the tobacco shop and a booming black market

children.content: A councillor from the Upper Hunter in NSW manages a tobacco shop that sells illegal cigarettes as experts warn the black-market crisis is deepening in regional areas.

gallery.altText: A board featuring a bearded man and the words "vote 1 for councillor" outside a shop.

thumbnail.altText: A board featuring a bearded man and the words "vote 1 for councillor" outside a shop.

avatar.alt: Photo of ABC Newcastle investigative reporter Alex Turner-Cohen, smiling and wearing a white shirt, arms folded

items.title: US B-52 bomber plunged into ground at more than 1,500m per minute

children.content: A B-52 bomber aircraft that crashed at a US Air Force base in California, killing eight people aboard, made a sharp right and then nearly completed a 180-degree turn before plunging to the ground.

image.altText: A long black burn mark on an air base runway smouldering with black smoke near parked vehicles.

items.title: Russian artist who satirised Putin shot dead in Poland

children.content: Two Belarusian nationals are arrested in connection with the killing, authorities say.

image.altText: A man wearing a hat at a demonstration on a street.

items.title: 'Life-changing' school for autistic children saved from 'chopping block'

children.content: The private Sycamore School in Alexandra Hills is so sought after, some parents drive almost 50 kilometres one-way to drop off their kids, but it was "on the chopping block".

avatar.alt: A journalist holding a microphone and smiling.
